Section:
Overview
A hyperplane arrangement is a finite collection of affine or linear hyperplanes in a vector space. Hyperplane arrangements appear in many areas of mathematics including
combinatorics,
algebraic geometry,
reflection groups,
and topology.
The HypArr package provides basic functionality for working with hyperplane arrangements in GAP.

Representation of Arrangements
In this package, a hyperplane arrangement is represented by a list of vectors describing the defining linear forms.
If r = (r_1,\dots,r_n) is such a vector, it corresponds to the hyperplane
r_1 x_1 + \dots + r_n x_n = 0.
Thus an arrangement
\mathcal{A} = \{H_1,\dots,H_m\}
is represented by the list
[ r1, r2, ..., rm ]
where each r_i defines the hyperplane H_i.

Intersection Lattices
A central combinatorial invariant of a hyperplane arrangement is its intersection lattice.
This lattice consists of all intersections of hyperplanes in the arrangement ordered by reverse inclusion.
The package provides the function to compute this lattice.
